mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar S-Type 2.7d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Jaguar S-Type 2.7d engine (AJD-V6) typically costs £3,500–£6,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,200–£1,600. An oil pump replacement costs £1,500–£3,000. The AJD-V6 is a specialist engine requiring Jaguar diesel expertise.

The AJD-V6 is refined and torquey, but has well-documented weaknesses. Crankshaft/big-end bearing failure and oil pump housing failure are the defining issues. Pre-2005 cars without a DPF are more reliable. Regular oil changes (5,000–7,500 miles) and timing belt changes (105,000 miles) are essential. A well-maintained example is as reliable as the big German diesels.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar S-Type 2.7d?

acceptedAnswer.text: Crankshaft/big-end bearing failure (cold-start knock, £4,000–£6,500), oil pump housing failure (oil pressure warnings, £1,500–£3,000), turbocharger failure leading to scored liners (£1,500–£3,500), and DPF/EGR issues on post-2005 models (£400–£1,200).

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Jaguar S-Type 2.7d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: S-Type 2.7d values range from £1,500–£6,000 depending on condition and mileage. A £3,500–£6,500 reconditioned engine represents 60–100% of vehicle value — our 60% Jaguar scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained, low-mileage example, replacement may be justified. For a higher-mileage example with other issues, assess scrap value first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Jaguar S-Type 2.7d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The S-Type 2.7d uses the AJD-V6 engine code (also known as 7B / AJTDV6). This is the Ford/PSA Lion V6 diesel, a 2.7-litre twin-turbo V6 producing 207bhp. The code is stamped on the engine block; your VIN and vehicle documentation will also confirm the specification.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the crankshaft bearing issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: In many cases, engine replacement is the more cost-effective option (£3,500–£6,500) compared to a bottom-end rebuild (£4,000–£6,500). If caught extremely early — before bearing damage has scored the crankshaft — a specialist may grind the crankshaft and fit oversized bearings. But for most examples, replacement is recommended. At the first sign of cold-start knock, stop driving immediately and book an inspection.

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an S-Type 2.7d engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £80–£120/hr (independent Jaguar specialist) or £140–£190/hr (London/SE dealer). The V6's transverse mount makes access tight.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Jaguar S-Type 2.7d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Only viable if caught extremely early; often cheaper to replace the engine
Viable — catches the fault before catastrophic bearing failure
Replacement often £3,500–£6,500 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Jaguar S-Type 2.7d? Check These First
buyIf: * Pre-2005 model (no DPF) — simpler emissions system, fewer failures
* Documented oil pump and timing belt history (belt due at 105,000 miles)
* Full service history with regular oil changes (5,000–7,500 miles)
* No cold-start knock or oil pressure warnings
avoidIf: * Any cold-start knock or rattle — bearing risk is real
* No oil pump history — the pump housing failure is common
* Post-2005 car with short-journey history — DPF issues almost certain
* Poor service history — the Lion V6 is unforgiving of extended oil changes

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The S-Type 2.7d is a refined, torquey diesel with genuine character — but it has well-documented weaknesses. The crankshaft bearing and oil pump failures are the defining issues of the Lion V6 family. Pre-2005 cars without a DPF are preferable. Buy on history, maintain the oil system religiously, and change the timing belt at 105,000 miles. A well-maintained example is a reliable, economical luxury saloon.

cta.label: → Compare Jaguar S-Type 2.7d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Jaguar S-Type 2.7d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any knock or rattle (bearing risk is the biggest warning)
* Service History — Oil change intervals are critical; 5,000–7,500 miles is safer than Jaguar's 12,000-mile schedule
* Timing Belt History — Belt due at 105,000 miles / 10 years; check if completed
* Oil Pressure — Any warnings? Oil pump housing failure is common
* DPF (Post-2005) — Ask about driving patterns; frequent short trips cause DPF issues
* Turbo Response — Test acceleration; any whistling or lag?
* Gearbox Behaviour — ZF 6HP26 can suffer faults; check for smooth changes
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Resolve before buying
